steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Transitions. The sentence before the blank explains that Roman numerals indicate naming order, not proximity. The sentence after the blank states a conclusion that follows directly from that fact — that assuming Helike is the 45th-closest moon would be incorrect. The transition must signal a logical consequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "however,": signals contrast or contradiction between two opposing ideas. The incorrect assumption flows predictably from the numbering system — it is a logical trap, not a counterpoint. ✗
  • Choice B — "finally,": signals the last item in a series or list. No series has been introduced; only one explanatory sentence precedes the blank. ✗
  • Choice C — "then,": signals a logical consequence meaning "given what was just stated, it follows that." Given that numerals indicate naming order, it follows that you should not assume proximity — a direct chain of reasoning. ✓
  • Choice D — "moreover,": signals an additional, reinforcing point. The sentence after the blank does not add new information; it states what should not be inferred from the rule just explained. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
